sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies Inc (KEYS) Q4 2025 Earnings Report - Results, Call & Slides
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations with an actual EPS of $1.91 against a consensus of $1.83 and actual revenue of $1.42 billion, beating the expected $1.38 billion. The company recorded a year-over-year revenue growth of 10.26%. The next earnings report is estimated for August 19, 2026.

feed.title: 12 Information Technology Stocks Moving In Thursday's Pre-Market Session
feed.summary: This article lists 12 information technology stocks that showed significant movement in Thursday's pre-market session. It details both gainers like Domo, NVE, and Richardson Electronics, with some reporting recent earnings, and losers such as STMicroelectronics and Abits Group. The report includes their respective stock price changes, current prices, and market capitalization.

feed.title: Brown & Brown partners with Anthropic, McKinsey and Accenture
feed.summary: Brown & Brown is embarking on an AI-first enterprise transformation, partnering with Anthropic, McKinsey & Company, and Accenture. This initiative aims to integrate AI across its operations, empowering its 23,000 teammates and deploying Anthropic’s Claude for various functions, including customer service and software development. The company emphasizes that AI will enhance employee capabilities rather than replace them, focusing on responsible implementation and specialized solutions.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial declares $1.70 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial, Inc. (NYSE:AMP) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.70 per common share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on August 21, 2026, to shareholders of record as of August 3, 2026. This decision was approved by the company’s Board of Directors.

feed.title: Union Pacific beats Q2 estimates on volume growth
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) exceeded Q2 adjusted earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $3.41 EPS against an estimated $3.19, and $6.9 billion in revenue against $6.65 billion. The strong performance was driven by volume growth and core pricing gains, leading to a 12% year-over-year revenue increase and a 6% rise in net income. The company also raised its 2026 EPS growth outlook to high-single digits, affirming its commitment to operational improvements and strong cash generation.

feed.title: Freeport Results Webcast Includes Slides, Replay Through Aug. 21
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (NYSE: FCX) has released its second-quarter and six-month 2026 financial results, which are available on its Investor Relations website. The company is hosting a conference call and webcast with slides for securities analysts today at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss these results. A replay of the webcast will be accessible until Friday, August 21, 2026.

feed.title: Cummins Announces Phased Model Year 2027 Product Launch Plan
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. announced a phased launch plan for its Model Year 2027 X10 and X15 engines, adapting to proposed changes in U.S. EPA emissions regulations. This strategy allows for a measured production ramp-up, maintaining regulatory compliance and supporting customer production schedules while keying on quality. The current X15, X12, and L9 engines will remain available during the transition, with the next-generation B platform scheduled for a 2028 launch.

feed.title: Farsight AI Tool Will Build Pitch Decks From Firm Templates
feed.summary: S&P Global Market Intelligence announced a strategic partnership and minority investment in Farsight to integrate an AI-powered client-ready material creation capability into its Capital IQ Pro platform. This new add-on, expected later in 2026, will utilize Farsight’s tools, Capital IQ Pro data, and a firm's own templates to generate tailored pitch decks, confidential information memoranda (CIMs), and valuation materials. The initiative supports Kensho Data & Platforms’ strategy for AI-native workflows, aiming to enhance efficiency for bankers and investors.

feed.title: Here Are Thursday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: AT&T, Applied Digital, Autodesk, Duke Energy, Exxon Mobil, JPMorgan Chase & Company, Live Nation Entertainment, and More
feed.summary: This article details Thursday's top Wall Street analyst research calls, including upgrades, downgrades, and new initiations for several major companies like AT&T, JPMorgan Chase, and Exxon Mobil. It also provides a snapshot of pre-market stock futures, bond yields, commodity prices (oil, gold), and cryptocurrency trends, highlighting ongoing concerns about inflation and geopolitical instability impacting markets.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on Keurig Dr Pepper to $38 From $33, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has raised its price target for Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) to $38 from $33, while reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the non-alcoholic beverage producer. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments for Keurig Dr Pepper.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor Q1 earnings seen lower; analysts revise targets
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corporation is projected to report lower first-quarter earnings, with analysts estimating 87 cents per share on revenue of $1.02 billion. The company will release its Q1 FY27 earnings report after the closing bell on Thursday, July 23. Several analysts have adjusted their price targets and ratings, with some upgrading the stock while others maintained or downgraded their positions.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics raises full-year guidance after Q2 revenue rises 10.2%
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics reported strong Q2 2026 financial results, with net revenue increasing by 10.2% to $3.043 billion and adjusted diluted EPS rising 19.1% to $3.12, both surpassing analyst estimates. Attributing the growth to execution across various channels and sustained demand, the company raised its full-year 2026 guidance for revenue to $11.95 billion–12.05 billion and adjusted diluted EPS to $11.05–$11.25, following a favorable resolution of tax contingencies. Operational highlights included advancing Co-Lab Solutions, new kidney care capabilities, and double-digit growth in Advanced Diagnostics like the Quest AD-Detect blood test.

feed.title: INVESTOR DEADLINE APPROACHING: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Insulet (PODD) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit Deadline on August 31, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential claims against Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) and reminds investors that the deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action lawsuit is August 31, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Insulet made false or misleading statements regarding its manufacturing controls and procedures, leading to product recalls and significant stock price drops. Investors who purchased Insulet securities between February 21, 2023, and May 26, 2026, may be eligible to participate.

feed.title: Dow tops Q2 forecasts, cautious outlook sends shares lower (DOW:NYSE)
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) exceeded Q2 revenue and earnings estimates, driven by higher polyethylene prices. However, shares fell in premarket trading due to a cautious outlook forecasting softer demand, lower volumes, and increased costs. The company is actively addressing these challenges by increasing its restructuring savings target to over $1.3 billion for 2026.

feed.title: Here's What to Expect From Regeneron Pharmaceuticals’ Next Earnings Report
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) is set to announce its fiscal Q2 2026 earnings on July 30, with analysts expecting a 25.4% drop in quarterly profit from the previous year. Despite underperforming the S&P 500 and the healthcare sector ETF over the past year, the company recently saw a boost from an expanded cancer therapy collaboration and positive drug trial results. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for REGN, projecting a 27.2% potential upside from current levels.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial (NYSE: AMP) Q2 earnings surge with 22% EPS growth
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with adjusted operating earnings per diluted share increasing by 22% to $11.07. The company's total assets under management, administration, and advisement grew 14% to a record $1.8 trillion, and it returned $932 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Both Advice & Wealth Management and Asset Management segments showed significant growth in pretax adjusted operating earnings.

feed.title: Union Pacific (NYSE: UNP) boosts Q2 profit and raises EPS growth outlook
feed.summary: Union Pacific reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with net income rising 6% to $1.993 billion and diluted EPS increasing 7% to $3.36. Adjusted diluted EPS grew 13% to $3.41, and total operating revenue increased 12% to $6.864 billion. The company also improved its 2026 EPS growth outlook to high-single digits and discussed its proposed acquisition of Norfolk Southern, noting associated risks.

feed.title: Roper Technologies stock rises 4% on beating estimates, raising guidance By Investing.com
feed.summary: Roper Technologies reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations for both adjusted earnings per share and revenue. The company also raised its full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance and increased its outlook for total and organic revenue growth, leading to a 4% rise in its stock. This positive performance was attributed to strong first-half results, share repurchases, and sustained customer demand for its specialized solutions.

feed.title: Wall Street set to extend losses after mixed earnings from Alphabet and Tesla
feed.summary: Wall Street is expected to open lower due to mixed earnings reports from Alphabet and Tesla, as well as renewed inflation and interest rate concerns stemming from a surge in oil prices. Alphabet's shares fell despite beating revenue and earnings forecasts, while Tesla experienced its first quarter of negative free cash flow in over two years. European markets also declined, and ongoing geopolitical tensions involving US, Iran, and Houthi strikes have driven crude oil prices higher, leading to fears of prolonged stagflation.

feed.title: Exclusive / Delta to launch direct flights to Saudi Arabia
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is set to launch direct flights to Saudi Arabia in October, marking the first time a US carrier has flown this route in 25 years. Despite initial softer-than-anticipated ticket sales due to the Iran conflict, Delta views this as temporary and believes Riyadh could become a regional safe haven for travel. The airline will use an Airbus A350 for the Atlanta to Riyadh service, starting on October 23rd, even as other airlines cancel their Gulf routes.

feed.title: Northrim's Proposed Merger Would Bring 11 Oregon Branches
feed.summary: Northrim BanCorp (NASDAQ: NRIM) has agreed to acquire PBCO Financial in an all-stock merger valued at approximately $167.3 million, or $32.36 per PBCO share. This acquisition will mark Northrim's first out-of-state expansion, adding 11 branches in Southern Oregon and the Willamette Valley. The combined entity is projected to have about $4.2 billion in assets, $3.0 billion in loans, and $3.5 billion in deposits, with the deal expected to close in Q4 2026 or early Q1 2027.

feed.title: American Tower (AMT) Stock Looks Reasonable Even With A 40% Value Gap
feed.summary: American Tower (AMT) stock appears undervalued based on a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model, suggesting a 40.4% discount to its estimated intrinsic value of $279 per share. Though its P/E ratio is above the industry average, it is below its peer group and the model's fair P/E, also indicating undervaluation. The article emphasizes that the key for investors is whether American Tower can successfully execute its tower and data center strategy to close this valuation gap.

feed.title: CSX (CSX) Stock Looks Overvalued Even After A Strong Run
feed.summary: CSX stock, despite a strong 67.5% return over five years, appears overvalued according to both Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and P/E multiples. The DCF model suggests a 17.6% premium, valuing the stock at $42.47 per share, while its P/E ratio of 30.4x is higher than justified by its earnings profile compared to peers. The article concludes that limited room for error exists if market sentiment or fundamentals decline.

feed.title: OptimizeRx appoints Sarah Bast as chief marketing officer By Investing.com
feed.summary: OptimizeRx Corp. (NASDAQ:OPRX) announced the appointment of Sarah Bast as Chief Marketing Officer. Bast brings over 20 years of pharmaceutical marketing experience, having previously served as Executive Vice President at Publicis Health Media and guided marketing strategy for major life sciences companies. In her new role, she will lead OptimizeRx's marketing strategy, brand positioning, and go-to-market initiatives, focusing on strengthening relationships with healthcare marketing agencies and life sciences teams.

feed.title: Applied Materials to Report Fiscal Third Quarter 2026 Results on Aug. 13, 2026
feed.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. announced that it will host its fiscal third quarter 2026 earnings conference call on Thursday, August 13, 2026. The call is scheduled for 4:30 p.m. ET / 1:30 p.m. PT and will be webcast live on the company's investor relations website, with a replay available by 8:00 p.m. ET / 5:00 p.m. PT on the same day. Applied Materials is a leader in materials engineering solutions crucial for semiconductor and advanced display manufacturing.

feed.title: T-Mobile US (TMUS) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: T-Mobile US (TMUS) reported a strong Q2 2026 with total revenues up 8% year-over-year to $22.8 billion and net income of $3.2 billion. The company highlighted significant acquisitions, including UScellular's wireless business, and achieved a record-high wireless NPS score. T-Mobile also raised its 2026 guidance for Core Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted Free Cash Flow, projecting continued growth and shareholder returns.

feed.title: Clark Atlanta University Establishes Endowed Sustainability Professorship with $500,000 Southern Company Grant
feed.summary: Clark Atlanta University (CAU) has established an endowed professorship in sustainability thanks to a $500,000 matching grant from the Southern Company Foundation. This grant, part of the HBCU Sustainable Communities Initiative, will fund a tenure-track faculty member focused on environmental politics, urban planning, and sustainable communities, strengthening CAU's academic offerings and leadership in environmental innovation. The endowment ensures permanent funding for research and educational experiences, attracting distinguished scholars and preparing students to address critical environmental and social challenges.

feed.title: Cigna Healthcare expands AI care programs to reach more customers
feed.summary: Cigna Healthcare is expanding its AI-enabled care management programs to reach 20% more customers with complex health needs, including cancer, heart disease, and high-risk pregnancy. These programs use predictive analytics to identify individuals who could benefit from earlier clinical support, connecting them with over 1,250 Cigna Healthcare clinicians. The company anticipates these initiatives will reduce medical costs by an average of $2,000 per engaged customer annually, totaling approximately $200 million in savings over three years, while also improving customer satisfaction and health outcomes like earlier cancer detection and reduced inpatient stays.

feed.title: Does Your ETF Portfolio Reflect Your Goals? A Look at the SPDR Global Stock Market ETF vs. the Climate Paris Aligned ETF.
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs from State Street: the SPDR Global Stock Market ETF (SPGM) and the Climate Paris Aligned ETF (NZAC). SPGM offers broad, low-cost global equity exposure with nearly 3,000 holdings, while NZAC provides specialized, ESG-screened global exposure focused on climate transition risks and opportunities with fewer holdings. Investors choose between these based on whether they prioritize maximum diversification and lower costs (SPGM) or a climate-conscious investment strategy (NZAC).

feed.title: Thermo Fisher stock rebounds after Q2 beat (TMO:NYSE)
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) saw its stock rebound by approximately 5% in premarket trading after reporting better-than-expected Q2 2026 results. This comes after a recent decline influenced by a peer's lower outlook. The life sciences company is based in Waltham, Massachusetts.

feed.title: BMO Announces Cash Distributions for Certain BMO ETFs and ETF Series of BMO Mutual Funds for July 2026
feed.summary: BMO Asset Management Inc. and BMO Investments Inc. have announced the July 2026 cash distributions for unitholders of BMO ETFs and ETF Series of BMO Mutual Funds. Unitholders of record by July 30, 2026, will receive distributions payable on August 5, 2026. The announcement includes a detailed table listing the per unit cash distribution amount for various funds.

feed.title: Saudi PIF's $55 billion EA deal approved under EU merger rules
feed.summary: A group of investors led by Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has received EU antitrust approval for its $55 billion acquisition of Electronic Arts. The European Commission found no competition concerns, though it is still scrutinizing the deal under its Foreign Subsidies Regulation (FSR), with a decision expected by July 30. This acquisition, also involving Jared Kushner's Affinity Partners and Silver Lake, is noted as the largest leveraged buyout in history.

feed.title: Old Dominion raises quarterly dividend by 3.6%
feed.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (NASDAQ:ODFL) announced a 3.6% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it to $0.29 per share of common stock. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on September 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of September 2, 2026. Old Dominion is a leading North American less-than-truckload motor carrier, offering various transportation and logistics services.

feed.title: Ciprocopan (NXP100) Receives Marketing Approval in China
feed.summary: Nuvectis Pharma, Inc. announced that its drug ciprocopan (NXP100), a once-daily oral complement Factor B inhibitor, has received marketing approval in China for patients with Paroxysmal Nocturnal Hemoglobinuria (PNH) untrained with complement inhibitors. This approval marks the world's first for such a drug, providing a new, effective, and convenient treatment option. The decision was based on a successful Phase 3 study showing ciprocopan's superiority over eculizumab in PNH patients.

feed.title: Nuvectis Drug Reached 12 g/dL in 59.5% of PNH Patients
feed.summary: Nuvectis Pharma announced that its once-daily oral Complement Factor B inhibitor, ciprocopan (NXP100), has received marketing approval in China for treating Paroxysmal Nocturnal Hemoglobinuria (PNH) patients previously untreated with complement inhibitors. This marks the first global approval for such a drug and is based on a Phase 3 trial where ciprocopan demonstrated superiority over Soliris, with 59.5% of patients achieving a hemoglobin target of 12 g/dL and 94.6% avoiding transfusions. Nuvectis holds exclusive rights to commercialize ciprocopan outside Greater China, India, and parts of Southeast Asia.

feed.title: Applied Materials to Report Fiscal Third Quarter 2026 Results on Aug. 13, 2026
feed.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. (Nasdaq: AMAT) announced it will hold its fiscal third quarter 2026 earnings conference call on Thursday, Aug. 13, 2026, at 4:30 p.m. ET / 1:30 p.m. PT. The call will be webcast live on its investor relations website, with a replay available later that day. Applied Materials is a leader in materials engineering solutions for semiconductor and advanced display manufacturing.

feed.title: Applied Materials sets Aug. 13 earnings webcast; replay by 8 p.m. ET
feed.summary: Applied Materials (NASDAQ: AMAT) announced it will hold its fiscal third quarter 2026 earnings conference call on Thursday, August 13, 2026, at 4:30 p.m. ET. A live webcast will be available on the company's investor relations website, with a replay accessible by 8:00 p.m. ET the same day. This announcement comes as the company continues to be a leader in materials engineering solutions for semiconductors and advanced displays.

feed.title: S&P 500 Futures Fall in Premarket Trading; Medpace Holdings, Allegion Lead
feed.summary: S&P 500 futures are falling in premarket trading. Medpace Holdings Inc. (MEDP) is up 17.4%, and Allegion PLC (ALLE) is up 14.8% ahead of the U.S. stock market open.

feed.title: Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON) Updates Q3 2026 Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON) has updated its Q3 2026 earnings guidance, projecting EPS of $2.05-$2.20 and revenue of $4.9 billion-$5.0 billion, which are both below Wall Street's consensus estimates. Despite this cautious outlook for the near future, the company reported a strong Q2, with EPS of $4.52 and revenue of $9.72 billion significantly exceeding expectations. Analysts have a mixed view on HON, with an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $257.79.

feed.title: Clark Atlanta University Establishes Endowed Sustainability Professorship with $500,000 Southern Company Grant
feed.summary: Clark Atlanta University has established an endowed professorship in sustainability, thanks to a $500,000 matching grant from the Southern Company Foundation. This initiative is part of the Historically Black Colleges and Universities Sustainable Communities Initiative (HBCU SCI) and aims to support a faculty member focused on environmental politics, urban planning, and sustainable communities. The endowment will allow the university to recruit a distinguished scholar, enhance academic offerings, and prepare students to address pressing environmental and social challenges.

feed.title: Sarah Bast Brings More Than Two Decades of Pharma Marketing to OptimizeRx
feed.summary: OptimizeRx (Nasdaq: OPRX) has appointed Sarah Bast as its new Chief Marketing Officer, effective July 23, 2026. Bast brings over two decades of experience in pharmaceutical marketing, specializing in point of care media strategy, analytics, and optimization. In her new role, she will lead OptimizeRx's marketing strategy, brand positioning, and go-to-market initiatives, aiming to strengthen relationships within the healthcare industry and support product innovation.

feed.title: Fastenal (FAST) Stock Could Be Fully Priced Following Strong Q2 Results
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) stock has delivered an 88.3% return over five years, but recent valuation checks suggest it may be fully priced. Trading at a P/E of 38.5x, well above industry averages and its fair P/E estimate of 29.8x, the stock appears overvalued despite strong Q2 2026 results. This valuation signals that much of the optimism for Fastenal's future growth and investments is already reflected in its current share price, leading to a tighter margin for error for investors.

feed.title: Graham Board of Adjustment won't waive ordinance to allow Dollar Tree sign larger than city allows
feed.summary: The Graham Board of Adjustment unanimously denied Dollar Tree's request for a sign 40% larger than the city's ordinance allows for its new store at 811 South Main Street. Board members found the presentation by Dollar Tree's representatives lacking in factual evidence, relying too much on opinion rather than specific data to justify the variance. The decision upholds the city's existing signage standards, despite arguments for increased visibility and customer safety.

feed.title: Westpac Banking Corp Trims Position in Xcel Energy Inc. $XEL
feed.summary: Westpac Banking Corp significantly reduced its stake in Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL) by 60.8% in the first quarter, selling 26,918 shares and retaining 17,374 shares worth approximately $1.38 million. Despite this, Wall Street analysts largely maintain a positive outlook on XEL, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average price target of $92.65, comfortably above its current trading price of around $80.19. Xcel Energy reported in-line quarterly earnings of $0.91 per share on revenues of $4.02 billion and continues to pay a quarterly dividend, offering a 3.0% annual yield.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Upgrades United Dominion Realty Trust (NYSE:UDR) to Strong-Buy
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has upgraded United Dominion Realty Trust (NYSE:UDR) to a "strong-buy" rating, despite a mixed reception from other analysts and the company missing its recent quarterly EPS estimates. UDR, a real estate investment trust specializing in multifamily apartment communities, also announced a 25 million-share buyback program and reaffirmed its FY 2026 guidance. The stock currently has an average "Hold" rating with an average price target of $41.50 across all analysts.

feed.title: JPMorgan Quants Say Strongest Earnings Upgrades Are in Europe
feed.summary: According to JPMorgan Chase & Co. quants, Europe is currently leading globally in corporate earnings upgrades. This trend indicates a rapidly improving European market for earnings per share, with analysts continuing to raise their growth expectations across the world, though Europe shows the sharpest increase.

feed.title: Blackstone Q2 earnings beat as strong inflows boost AUM (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) reported Q2 earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations, driven by substantial inflows of nearly $70 billion, which significantly boosted assets under management and subsequently increased management and advisory fees. The company's investment strategies showed strong performance, with infrastructure leading with a 7.2% return, followed by its Absolute Return multi-asset strategy at 5.8%. All major segments of Blackstone's operations experienced positive returns during the quarter.

feed.title: Cigna says AI tools to save customers $200 million in medical expenses over three years
feed.summary: Health insurer Cigna announced that its new AI tools are projected to save customers $200 million in medical costs over the next three years. These tools will identify patients with chronic or complex conditions earlier, connect them to clinicians, and boost clinical access by 20%. The company expects earlier detection of cancers and an annual saving of $2,000 for customers using these clinical programs.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares posts decline in Q2 margin on funding costs, 2026 outlook maintained (HBAN:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) reported a modest decline in its second-quarter margin due to increased funding costs. Despite this, the bank's revenue and earnings were in line with analyst expectations, and it maintained its full-year 2026 outlook. Following the news, HBAN shares were trading lower.

feed.title: Pool Corporation Reports Second Quarter Results; Confirms Annual Earnings Guidance Range, Excluding CEO Transition Costs
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (POOL) reported its second-quarter 2026 results, showing a 2% increase in net sales to $1.8 billion, driven by resilient maintenance business and improved building materials sales. The company confirmed its annual earnings guidance range of $10.87 to $11.17 per diluted share, excluding CEO transition costs. Operating income decreased 2% to $267.7 million but increased 1% to $275.9 million when excluding the CEO transition costs.
